title: Lonely Watchman
content:
City and beloved are far behind.
    I am so betrayed and alone.
    Slowly I move from one
    Leg to the other.
    Around me strange doors screech.
    I reach for dagger and gun.
    Ah, if I were only at home
    With my mother.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Alfred Lichtenstein delivers a powerful performance in "Lonely Watchman"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
